title: Dragonlight Router — Implementation Delta & Remediation Plan
description: Gap analysis between current implementation and live-spec acceptance criteria, plus coding standards compliance audit. Produces actionable remediation tasks for parallel agent delegation.

data.title: MBR (Model Based Router) — capability filtering stage

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: MBR filters candidates to those matching DispatchOrder.complexity_tier OR one tier above
MBR excludes backends with circuit_open status
MBR performs graceful upgrade to next tier when zero candidates in requested tier
MBR NEVER downgrades — complex request never routed to medium/haiku tier
MBR includes local providers as unlimited-rate in LBR passthrough

data.remediation: NEW_MODULE — create selection/mbr.py with filter_by_capability(), estimate_complexity(), adjacent-tier upgrade logic

data.title: CBR (Cost Balancing Router) — cost scoring stage

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: CBR filters providers with zero budget remaining
CBR scores by projected cost with budget-pressure weight shifting
CostGovernor activates when daily_spend > 80% of daily_budget
cost_adjusted_weights shifts weight from rank to budget as pressure increases

data.remediation: NEW_MODULE — create selection/cbr.py with CostGovernorConfig, cost_governor_active(), cost_adjusted_weights()

data.title: LBR (Load Balancing Router) — rate-limit-aware dispatch

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: LBR filters providers with no RPM/RPD/TPM capacity remaining
LBR enforces daily token cap (0 = unlimited)
LBR uses WFQ-inspired scheduling across surviving candidates
LBR tracks RPM, RPD, TPM, and daily token cap per provider

data.remediation: NEW_MODULE — create selection/lbr.py with rate-limit-aware filtering and WFQ scheduling. BudgetTracker._rpm_remaining() provides partial RPM logic but TPM and daily token cap are untracked.

data.title: Cascade Dispatch — MBR→CBR→LBR composition

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: route() composes MBR→CBR→LBR in fixed order
Each stage returns Result[CandidateSet, StageError]
Empty candidate set at any stage returns Err with diagnostics
route() passes full context through all three stages
Fallback chain constructed from surviving candidates after LBR

data.remediation: NEW_MODULE — create dispatch/cascade.py with route() function composing the three stages

data.title: Provider Adapters — 8 provider implementations

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: Each adapter implements GenerativeBackend protocol
All adapters use OpenAI-compatible wire format
Adapters for: nvidia, groq, anthropic, openai, mistral, deepinfra, together, ollama
Each adapter handles provider-specific auth (env key, header)
Adapters surface provider-specific rate limit headers

data.remediation: NEW_MODULES — create adapters/{nvidia,groq,anthropic,openai,mistral,deepinfra,together,ollama}.py each implementing GenerativeBackend protocol

data.title: Context Trust Tier Filtering — DIAN CECHT

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: filter_by_trust_tier() removes models that cannot serve the required trust tier
LOCAL tier trusts all tiers; OPUS trusts only OPUS
Untrusted providers never receive PII context
Trust tier derived from DispatchOrder fields

data.title: Canonical ScoringWeights + CostGovernor

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: compute_composite_score(rank, budget, health) with simple weights exists
ScoringWeights frozen dataclass with canonical 6-dimension weights exists
score_candidate() with normalization exists
CostGovernorConfig + cost_governor_active() exists
cost_adjusted_weights() shifts weight from rank to budget under pressure

data.remediation: GRAFT — add ScoringWeights, score_candidate(), CostGovernorConfig, cost_governor_active(), cost_adjusted_weights() to existing scoring.py alongside compute_composite_score()

data.title: Health Check Loop — periodic backend probing

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: Background task probes each backend on configurable interval
Probes update BackendState.status based on response
Retired backends (404) are permanently excluded
Check loop integrates with CircuitBreaker state machine

data.remediation: NEW_MODULE — create health/check_loop.py. Existing CircuitBreaker and HealthTracker provide partial foundation but no periodic probing exists.

data.title: HTTP API Dispatch Endpoints

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: POST /v1/select endpoint exists
POST /v1/record endpoint exists
GET /v1/health endpoint exists
POST /v1/dispatch endpoint exists
POST /v1/retire endpoint exists
POST /v1/reinstate endpoint exists
All error responses use structured JSON (not stack traces)
Input validation at system boundaries

data.remediation: GRAFT — add POST /v1/dispatch, POST /v1/retire, POST /v1/reinstate to routes.py. Fix error handling (except Exception → specific exceptions). Add input validation at boundaries.

data.title: RouterEngine.dispatch() graft

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: RouterEngine.select_models() works correctly
RouterEngine.dispatch(order: DispatchOrder) -> EngineResponse | DispatchFailure exists
dispatch() delegates to dispatch.cascade.route()
dispatch() is fully backward compatible with select_models()

data.remediation: GRAFT — add dispatch() method to RouterEngine class. Also fix select_models() which is 71 lines (hard limit: 40).

data.title: Integration Tests — cascade dispatch pipeline

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: Full MBR→CBR→LBR→dispatch pipeline exercises real routing
Circuit breaker triggers correctly under failure load
Fallback chain advances when primary fails
Budget enforcement prevents overspend
Provider catalog refresh produces correct model list

data.title: BudgetTracker TPM + Daily Token Cap

acceptance_criteria_status.criterion: RPM tracking via sliding window exists
RPD tracking via daily counter exists
TPM (tokens-per-minute) tracking exists
Daily token cap enforcement exists
has_capacity() checks RPM, RPD, TPM, and token cap
score() incorporates TPM ratio in composite

data.remediation: MODIFY — add TPM sliding window and daily token cap tracking to BudgetTracker. Add _tpm_windows, _tokens_today. Extend has_capacity() and score().

violations.remediation: Extract tier classification logic into _classify_tier() helper
Introduce RequestOutcome frozen dataclass and accept that
Group params into CacheKeyInput frozen dataclass
Catch json.JSONDecodeError specifically for JSON parse

violations.context: except OSError: pass inside except Exception block (SIM105)
violations.remediation: Use contextlib.suppress(OSError) for cleanup, and return Err(StatePersistenceError(...)) for the outer block

violations.justification: Registry is mutable by design (register, get) — but needs deviation record
violations.remediation: Add deviation record OR refactor to frozen + separate mutable BackendRegistryState

violations.justification: Runtime state must be mutable — valid exception per coding standard
violations.remediation: Add deviation record with explicit justification + mitigations

violations.context: Function missing type annotation for parameter
violations.remediation: Add type annotation to all app.py function parameters

violations.context: Missing type arguments for generic type dict
Returning Any from function declared to return str | None
violations.remediation: Add explicit type cast or narrow return type

violations.context: Missing type arguments for generic type dict
Returning Any / Missing type arguments for dict
violations.remediation: Add proper type annotations for dict and return values

violations.context: Missing type arguments for dict / Returning Any
violations.remediation: Add dict[str, Any] annotations and explicit casts

violations.context: Incompatible types in assignment (list | BaseException -> list)
violations.remediation: Add proper type narrowing with isinstance check before assignment

violations.context: Function missing type annotation for parameter

violations.remediation: Combine if branches with logical or
Use set literal instead of set(generator)
Use invariant() function or add noqa with deviation record

advisory_rules.remediation: Add precondition/postcondition assertions to all non-trivial functions. Prioritize: scoring.py, tracker.py, router.py, complexity.py

target_modules: selection/interleave.py — invariant: output length == input length, max_consecutive preserved
selection/complexity.py — invariant: tier never downgrades for high-complexity input
budget/tracker.py — idempotency: score() is idempotent between requests
caching/simple.py — idempotency: get(set(k,v)) == v, TTL expiration
advisory_rules.remediation: Add Hypothesis-based test files for each target module
Add structlog logging to all operational modules (circuit_breaker, routes, scoring, interleave, complexity, tracker, caching modules)

advisory_rules.status: ZERO Ok/Err Result types used anywhere in codebase
affected_functions: BudgetTracker.score() returns float — should return Result[float, ProviderNotFoundError]
HealthTracker.score() returns float — should return Result[float, ModelNotFoundError]
CatalogCache.get() returns dict | None — should return Result[dict, StaleCatalogError]
RouterEngine._refresh_catalog() — swallows exception via except Exception
advisory_rules.remediation: Introduce canonical Ok/Err Result type to core/types.py. Migrate all fallible operations to return Result.

missing_test_files.note: No dispatch tests — module doesn't exist yet
No adapter tests — modules don't exist yet

data.title: Decompose RouterEngine.select_models() from 71 to ≤40 lines
data.required_state: select_models() ≤ 40 lines, decomposed into helper functions
acceptance_criteria: select_models() body ≤ 40 lines
All extracted helpers have type annotations and return Result types
All existing tests pass unchanged
New helper functions have precondition assertions
data.agent_instructions: Refactor RouterEngine.select_models() into 3-4 smaller private methods. Each method must be independently testable. Preserve the exact public behavior. Add assertions per coding standard.

data.title: Fix 7 except Exception violations — replace with specific exception types
changes: cache.py:61 — except Exception → except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError, ValueError)
routes.py:55 — except Exception → except (KeyError, TypeError, ValueError)
persistence.py:38 — except Exception → except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError)
acceptance_criteria: Zero `except Exception` in codebase (confirmed by ruff/grep)
Each catch block handles specific exception types appropriate to the operation
No silent swallowing — every handler either returns Err, logs, or re-raises
All existing tests pass
data.agent_instructions: Replace each `except Exception` with the specific exception types listed. Ensure each handler either returns an Err Result, logs with structlog, or re-raises. Do NOT catch Exception anywhere. Run ruff to verify.

data.title: Introduce canonical Result type (Ok/Err) for all fallible operations
changes: Add Ok[T], Err[E], Result[T,E] to core/types.py per canonical pattern
acceptance_criteria: Ok/Err frozen dataclasses exist in core/types.py
All 7 target functions return Result type
Callers use isinstance(x, Ok) / isinstance(x, Err) for dispatch
No function returns both None and a value — None cases become Err
Hypothesis test: Ok.value and Err.error are always present
data.agent_instructions: Add the canonical Result type to core/types.py first. Then migrate each function one at a time, updating callers to use isinstance dispatch. Every Result-returning function needs both Ok and Err test cases.
data.notes: Result type introduced in core/types.py but many callers not updated to handle Ok/Err returns, causing test failures. Result type introduced but many callers/tests not updated, causing 44 test failures.

data.title: Fix mypy strict violations (13 errors)
changes: registry.py:41,43 — add dict[str, GenerativeBackend] and dict[str, BackendState] type params
caching/simple.py:48 — add explicit cast for Any return from sqlite3
caching/simple.py:68 — add dict[str, Any] type param
catalog/cache.py:78,83,94 — add proper dict type params, fix Any return
budget/persistence.py:18,47,60 — add dict type params, fix Any return
catalog/refresher.py:44 — add isinstance narrow before assignment
acceptance_criteria: mypy --strict src/ produces zero errors
pip install types-PyYAML added to project deps or dev deps
All existing tests pass
data.agent_instructions: Fix each mypy error by adding proper type annotations. Run mypy after each fix to confirm. Install types-PyYAML. Target: mypy src/ — zero errors.

data.title: Fix ruff violations (17 errors) and add missing docstrings
changes: Add S101 noqa or use invariant() for non-test assert
acceptance_criteria: ruff check src/ produces zero errors
All auto-fixable issues applied via ruff --fix
Remaining issues manually resolved
All existing tests pass
data.agent_instructions: Run ruff check src/ --fix first for auto-fixable items. Then manually fix remaining items. Run ruff check src/ to confirm zero errors.

data.title: Add precondition/postcondition assertions to all 93 unasserted functions
acceptance_criteria: Every non-trivial function has ≥1 precondition assertion
Functions that compute values have ≥1 postcondition assertion
Assertion messages are descriptive: assert x > 0, 'rpm_limit must be positive'
state.py invariant() function is used for critical invariants
data.agent_instructions: Add precondition assertions at function entry for all parameter constraints. Add postcondition assertions before return for computed values. Use the pattern: assert condition, 'descriptive message'. Follow state.py invariant() for critical checks.

data.title: Add Hypothesis property-based tests for 6 target modules
changes: Create 6 new Hypothesis test files with @given decorators
acceptance_criteria: 6 new test files with Hypothesis @given strategies
Each file has ≥3 properties testing different categories (roundtrip, invariant, idempotency)
All property tests pass with 100+ examples per property
Property docstrings document which of the 7 canonical categories they test
Tests run in <1s each
data.agent_instructions: Create Hypothesis test files following the dragonlight-property-based-testing-strategy.md. Each property test must document its category (round-trip, invariant preservation, idempotency, commutativity, subset, monotonicity, no-counterexample). Use 100+ examples. Use assume() to filter invalid inputs.

data.title: Add structlog logging to 17 modules currently without logging
changes: Add `import structlog; logger = structlog.get_logger()` to each module
Add logger.info() at function entry for state-changing operations
Add logger.error() for failures before returning Err
Event names in snake_case, include correlation fields
acceptance_criteria: All operational modules use structlog
Log entries include event name, relevant IDs, typed fields
No sensitive data (API keys, PII) in log output
Logging does not change function behavior
data.agent_instructions: Add structlog to each listed module. Log at entry of state-changing functions. Use snake_case event names. Include model_id, provider_name, or other context fields. Never log env_key values or request content.

data.title: Add deviation records for 2 non-frozen dataclasses
changes: Add DEVIATION_RECORD docstring to BackendRegistry class
Add DEVIATION_RECORD docstring to BackendState class
Record: rule violated, justification, approved by, mitigations, scope, expiration
acceptance_criteria: Each non-frozen dataclass has a deviation record in its docstring
Deviation records contain all 6 required fields
Deviation records reference the specific coding standard rule
data.agent_instructions: Add a DEVIATION_RECORD section to the class docstring of BackendRegistry and BackendState. Include: rule_violated, justification, approved_by, mitigations, scope, expiration. BackendState is justified as runtime mutable state. BackendRegistry needs justification for mutable dict defaults.

data.title: Fix record_request 5-parameter violation
changes: Create RequestOutcome frozen dataclass with provider, model_id, success, tokens_used, latency_ms
Update all callers in server/routes.py and tests
acceptance_criteria: record_request() has ≤4 parameters
RequestOutcome is frozen dataclass with all required fields
All callers updated
All tests pass
data.agent_instructions: Create a RequestOutcome frozen dataclass in core/types.py. Change record_request signature to accept it. Update routes.py record_handler and test_router_engine.py.

data.title: Fix 3 missing-assertion test functions
changes: test_unknown_provider_no_error — add assert on return value or state
test_record_success — add assert verifying health/budget state changed
test_record_failure — add assert verifying error count incremented
acceptance_criteria: All 3 test functions contain at least one assert or pytest.raises
Tests verify actual behavior, not just 'does not raise'
data.agent_instructions: Add meaningful assertions to each of the 3 test functions. For test_record_success, verify health_tracker.score() returns expected value. For test_record_failure, verify error_count. For test_unknown_provider, verify score() returns 100.0.

data.title: Add TPM + daily token cap tracking to BudgetTracker
changes: Add _tpm_windows: dict[str, deque[float]] sliding window (same pattern as RPM)
Extend has_capacity() to check RPM + RPD + TPM + token_cap
Extend score() to include TPM ratio in composite
Extend record_request() to track tokens in TPM window and daily token counter
acceptance_criteria: TPM sliding window tracking works identically to RPM tracking
Daily token cap enforced — has_capacity() returns False when cap reached
score() returns min(rpm_ratio, rpd_ratio, tpm_ratio, token_ratio) * 100
0 token_cap means unlimited (ratio=1.0)
Unit tests for all new capacity checks
Property test: score() in [0, 100] for all valid inputs
data.agent_instructions: Add TPM sliding window to BudgetTracker following the exact pattern of RPM tracking. Add daily token cap counter following RPD pattern. Extend has_capacity() and score() to include these new dimensions. The ProviderConfig already has tpm_limit field. Add comprehensive tests.

levels.description: Independent hard-rule fixes — no dependencies, all parallelizable
levels.notes: 8 tasks, 4 agents, 2 waves. RT-002/RT-004/RT-005/RT-010 in wave 1; RT-009/RT-011/RT-017/RT-019 in wave 2.

levels.description: Result type introduction — unblocks all cascade stages
levels.notes: Sequential — all downstream depends on Ok/Err being in core/types.py

levels.description: Function decomposition + canonical scoring + logging + TPM
levels.notes: 7 tasks, 4 agents. RT-001/RT-012 in wave 1 (critical path). RT-006/RT-007/RT-008/RT-018/RT-023 in wave 2.

levels.description: MBR stage — unblocks CBR and cascade
levels.notes: Sequential — CBR depends on MBR output format
Sequential — LBR depends on CBR output format
Sequential — cascade depends on all three stages

levels.description: Cascade composition + dispatch graft + routes + coverage
levels.notes: RT-016 first, then RT-020/RT-021 parallel, then RT-024
Must come after all implementation tasks

risk_assessment.highest_risk: RT-003 (Result type migration) — touches 7 files, cascading API changes
medium_risk: RT-001 (select_models decomposition) — must preserve exact behavior

acceptance_gate.description: All remediation complete when ALL of the following hold:
gates: All 12 TM-* acceptance criteria in live-spec met
Result type used by all fallible operations
Hypothesis property tests exist for 6 target modules
structlog used by all operational modules

data.purpose: Address test failures resulting from RT-003 (Result type introduction)
data.problem: Tests and callers expect raw values but now receive Ok/Err Result types
solution: Update all test files to unwrap Result types before assertions
Update any remaining caller code in server/routes.py and elsewhere
Ensure JSON serialization compatibility for API endpoints
Add proper error handling tests for Err cases

Update config loader tests to handle Result[RouterConfig, ConfigError]

hard_rules.rule: Every test has assert or pytest.raises

advisory_rules.rule: Canonical Result type for fallible ops

rules.rule: No module-level mutable state in imports
